{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |St. George Defence| |rd|nd| |qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= | |bd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= |pd| | | | | | | |= | |pd| | | | | | |= | | | |pl|pl| | | |= | | | |bl| |nl| | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl| | |rl|= || }} = St. George Defence = At that point, Black will usually play e6 and Nf6 in any order. [[/4...Nf6/]] adds up pressure and forces 5. Qe2 (5. e5 would free the bishop line of fire). [[/4...e6/]] builds Black pawn center and takes control of key squares. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. '1.e4 a6 2.d4 b5 3.Nf3 Bb7 4.Bd3'
